title: Unique Kenyan Sloe Berries Hash
description: A fermented, crumbly Kenyan dish featuring sloe berries, flambe, and dash-friendly ingredients, traditionally served as a hangover cure. This recipe combines the flavors of Africa with a twist of European flair.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the sloe berries, potatoes, onions, and garlic.
In a small bowl, whisk together the vegetable oil, flambe liqueur, salt, black pepper, and dash powder.
Pour the wet ingredients over the dry ingredients and stir until everything is well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a large skillet or frying pan and cook over medium heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Use a spatula to press the mixture down into the pan and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the bottom is crispy and golden brown.
Flip the hash over and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the other side is also crispy and golden brown.
Remove the hash from the heat and stir in the chopped scallions.
Let the hash cool to room temperature before serving.
To serve, cut the hash into wedges and serve with a fried egg or toast.
This dish can be made ahead of time and refrigerated or frozen for later use.
